_meta.dna: fan(system) = contribution(path) x recognition(visible) x trust(contractual) x voice(closed_loop) x story(honest)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions per floor. Prioritize questions that reveal fan acquisition blockers, community infrastructure gaps, and viral growth failures. Use the hardest question as the quality bar — if it cannot be answered honestly, the floor fails.
_meta.aggregate_prior: Fan Story A, Fan Infrastructure F (37.7% floor pass rate, Boss 48: 3/7, Boss 49: 7/7)
systemic_failures: SF1: Zero community infrastructure (CRITICAL — no Discord, no Slack, no forum, no mailing list)
SF2: No contributor leaderboard or recognition surface (HIGH — contribution loop open)
SF3: Vote endpoint not implemented (MEDIUM — returns HTTP 501)
SF4: No SLA or API stability contract (MEDIUM — trust asserted, not contractual)
SF5: No binary installer (HIGH — third tower in a row, 60% of fans blocked)
SF6: Selector fragility (MEDIUM — CSS selectors break when sites update DOM)
SF7: No onboarding path for non-technical fans (HIGH — terminal-first excludes largest audience)
SF8: Solo founder bus factor 1 (STRUCTURAL — all trust reduces to continuity risk)

name: Tower of Fans
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT(100q) -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

floors.score_prior: 8/10 (STRONG — local-first architecture resonates deeply)
questions: Is the local-first architecture genuinely zero-telemetry verifiable from source code, not just claimed?
Does the AES-256-GCM vault protect all credentials at rest with no plaintext fallback?
Can a privacy fan verify no data leaves the machine without explicit per-action consent?
Is there a transparency report or privacy audit documenting exactly what data is handled and how?
Does BYOK mean API keys are never transmitted to Solace servers under any code path?
Can a privacy fan audit the complete data flow from user action to evidence entry?
Is there a shareable privacy audit result or open-source verification tool that converts other privacy-conscious users?
Is the evidence chain local-only by default, with cloud sync requiring explicit opt-in?
Can a user delete all local data and have confidence the deletion is complete and verifiable?
Does the vault support multiple separate vaults for different security contexts?
Is the OAuth3 token stored in memory only or does it persist to disk in any code path?
Can a privacy fan run Solace in a network-isolated environment with zero outbound connections?
Does the product pass the Privacy Fan referral test: 'I would recommend this to a fellow privacy advocate without caveats'?
Is there a documented threat model that identifies what data is protected and from whom?
What is the hardest privacy question a fan could ask, and does the codebase answer it honestly?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full data sovereignty domain. Prioritize questions that expose gaps between privacy claims and actual code behavior. Fan perspective: 'Would I recommend this to my privacy-conscious friends?'

floors.score_prior: 7/10 (GOOD — recipe replay at $0 is genuinely remarkable; missing public gallery)
questions: Can a recipe fan browse, search, and rate recipes publicly without an account?
Is recipe replay at $0 genuinely remarkable enough to tell a friend about in one sentence?
Does the recipe format support community contribution and remixing without code knowledge?
Is there a public recipe gallery or marketplace where fans can discover new automations?
Can a fan track how much money recipe replay has saved them (vs. LLM cost per run)?
Is the recipe creation process accessible to users who have never used a terminal?
Can a recipe fan share their best recipe with a unique URL and have it load immediately?
Are recipes versioned so fans can see improvements over time and revert if a version breaks?
Is there a recipe health dashboard showing which selectors have broken on which sites?
Can a fan contribute a recipe without needing a GitHub account or git knowledge?
Does the recipe format documentation explain WHY the format is designed this way (not just HOW)?
Can a recipe fan see which recipes are most popular by play count across the community?
Is there a 'recipe of the week' or featured automation that surfaces new fan contributions?
Does recipe replay work offline, or does it require network access even for cached operations?
What is the hardest recipe limitation a power user would discover, and is it documented honestly?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full automation replay domain. Prioritize questions that reveal sharing friction, community discovery gaps, and recipe ecosystem maturity. Fan perspective: 'Is this remarkable enough to tell a friend?'

floors.score_prior: 7/10 (GOOD — ALCOA+ chains real; missing export and visualization)
questions: Can a data fan export their entire evidence history to a standard format (JSON, CSV, SQLite)?
Is there a visualization tool or dashboard showing evidence chain history over time?
Does the evidence chain spec allow third-party tools to read and verify the chain independently?
Can a fan verify the integrity of their evidence chain without relying on Solace tooling?
Is the evidence chain format documented as an open standard that others could implement?
Can a data fan query their evidence history with filters (date range, action type, app)?
Is there a diff view showing what changed between two evidence entries in a chain?
Does the evidence chain capture failed actions as well as successful ones (no survivorship bias)?
Can a fan share a specific evidence chain with a colleague without exposing their full vault?
Is there an evidence analytics mode showing patterns: most common actions, failure rates, time trends?
Does the ALCOA+ compliance documentation explain each principle (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate, Complete, Consistent, Enduring, Available) in plain language?
Can a data fan import evidence from external systems to create a unified audit trail?
Is there a size estimate tool showing how much storage evidence accumulates per year?
Does the evidence format support annotations or notes from the user alongside automatic entries?
What is the hardest evidence integrity question, and does the implementation answer it with code?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full evidence ownership domain. Prioritize questions that expose gaps between ALCOA+ claims and actual user-facing capabilities. Fan perspective: 'Can I own, verify, and use my own data?'

floors.score_prior: 6/10 (DECENT — CLI-first, BYOK, extensible; missing plugin API, SDK, dev community)
questions: Is there a public SDK or library that a developer can import to build on Solace programmatically?
Does the API have code examples in multiple languages (Python, JavaScript, cURL) beyond just OpenAPI?
Can a developer extend Solace with custom skills without forking the core codebase?
Is there a developer community (Discord channel, forum thread, mailing list) where devs share extensions?
Does the CLI support scripting mode for integration into CI/CD pipelines without interactive prompts?
Is there a Postman collection or Bruno collection for rapid API exploration?
Can a developer contribute a recipe via a pull request without deep knowledge of the internals?
Is the recipe format machine-readable enough for programmatic generation (not just hand-editing)?
Does the evidence chain have a library for reading/verifying chains in Python without Solace installed?
Is there a changelog with semantic versioning so developers can track breaking changes?
Can a developer register a webhook to be notified when a recipe completes or fails?
Does the API authentication support multiple auth methods (token, API key, OAuth3 scope) for different use cases?
Is the internal architecture (Triple-Twin, Swarm Router, evidence chain) documented well enough to extend without reading source?
Does Solace work in headless/server mode for automation tasks without a UI?
What is the hardest developer integration scenario, and does it work end-to-end without hacks?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full developer productivity domain. Prioritize questions that expose SDK gaps, documentation completeness, and community developer support. Fan perspective: 'Would I recommend this to a developer friend as a platform to build on?'

floors.score_prior: 5/10 (WEAK — YAML recipe editing is not visual; terminal is a hard blocker)
questions: Can a NoCode fan create a recipe from scratch without ever opening a terminal or editing YAML?
Is there a drag-and-drop recipe builder or visual flow editor for non-technical users?
Does the onboarding flow explain what Solace does using only visual metaphors (no CLI terms)?
Can a NoCode fan install Solace with a single double-click, no terminal required?
Is there a template gallery where NoCode fans can start from existing recipes without modification?
Can a NoCode fan test a recipe in a sandbox before applying it to real accounts?
Does the error message system use plain language that a non-technical user understands?
Is there a getting-started video (under 3 minutes) that shows the complete workflow visually?
Can a NoCode fan pause, edit, and resume a running recipe without knowing the underlying format?
Is the recipe sharing mechanism point-and-click, not git-based?
Does the UI explain what each recipe step will do BEFORE it runs, in everyday language?
Can a NoCode fan set up scheduled automations using a calendar picker, not a cron string?
Is there a mobile-friendly interface for monitoring running automations without a desktop?
Does the product have a NoCode-specific getting started path (vs. a developer path and a power user path)?
What is the first terminal command a NoCode fan would hit, and has it been eliminated from the critical path?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full visual automation domain. Prioritize questions that expose every point where a non-technical user would be blocked by terminal requirements. Fan perspective: 'Could I set this up for my non-technical parent?'

floors.score_prior: 5/10 (WEAK — keyboard nav partial; missing WCAG 2.1 AA audit, high contrast)
questions: Does the product have a current WCAG 2.1 AA audit report with named pass/fail checkpoints?
Is there a high-contrast mode that works without requiring custom OS-level themes?
Can a screen reader user complete the full workflow (install, run recipe, review evidence) without mouse?
Are all interactive elements reachable and activatable with keyboard alone?
Does the UI avoid relying on color alone to convey status (error, success, warning)?
Are form labels explicitly associated with their inputs (not just visually proximate)?
Does the product support font size scaling up to 200% without horizontal scrolling?
Are time-limited interactions (session timeouts, approval dialogs) adjustable for slow readers?
Is there an accessibility statement on solaceagi.com documenting current compliance level?
Does the recipe execution dialog provide screen reader announcements for status changes?
Can a user with motor disabilities use switch access or voice control to approve actions?
Are error messages associated with the form field that caused the error via aria-describedby?
Does the CLI output support machine-readable mode (JSON flags) for screen reader users who prefer programmatic access?
Is there an accessibility feedback email or channel so fans can report barriers?
What is the hardest accessibility barrier in the current product, and is it on the roadmap?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full inclusive design domain. Use WCAG 2.1 AA as the standard. Fan perspective: 'Would I feel comfortable recommending this product to a friend who uses assistive technology?'

floors.score_prior: 5/10 (WEAK — 81 recipes cover basics; missing conditional logic, branching, loops)
questions: Can an AutomateEverything fan write a recipe with conditional branching (if/else logic)?
Does the recipe system support loops (repeat N times, repeat until condition)?
Can recipes call other recipes as sub-routines for modular workflow design?
Is there a recipe debugger that shows which step failed and why, with enough context to fix it?
Can a fan build a multi-step workflow spanning multiple apps (Gmail -> Sheets -> Slack)?
Does the recipe system support variables that persist across steps in a single run?
Can a fan trigger a recipe from an external webhook (not just manual or scheduled)?
Is there a recipe error-handling mechanism (on_failure: retry / skip / abort)?
Can a fan compose recipes from community-submitted building blocks (sub-recipes as libraries)?
Does the recipe system support parallel execution of independent steps for speed?
Is there a visual recipe flow validator that catches logic errors before execution?
Can an AutomateEverything fan build a recipe that spans multiple browser sessions (multi-account)?
Does the platform expose a way to monitor 20+ scheduled recipes simultaneously in a dashboard?
Is there a recipe complexity limit, and if so, is it documented and justified?
What is the most complex recipe currently in the library, and what does it reveal about the ceiling?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full workflow maximalism domain. Focus on discovering the ceiling of the recipe system — what is impossible vs. just undocumented. Fan perspective: 'Can I automate my entire digital life with this?'

floors.score_prior: 4/10 (WEAK — local-first correct; missing Docker, Helm, systemd, one-liner deploy)
questions: Is there an official Docker image published to Docker Hub or GHCR with semantic versioning?
Does a Docker Compose setup exist for running the full stack (CLI + backend + browser) locally?
Is there a Helm chart for Kubernetes deployment with documented values.yaml?
Does the product have a systemd service file for running as a background daemon on Linux?
Is there a one-liner install script (curl | bash) with clear security caveats?
Can a self-hoster verify the Docker image SHA-256 against a published manifest?
Does the product support running behind a reverse proxy (nginx/Caddy/Traefik) with documented config?
Is there a minimal resource requirements spec (CPU/RAM/disk) for self-hosting at scale?
Can a self-hoster configure all settings via environment variables (twelve-factor app)?
Does the product have a health check endpoint for use by load balancers and monitoring?
Is there a self-hosting guide that covers backup, restore, and upgrade procedures?
Can a self-hoster contribute back to the community without depending on GitHub access?
Does the product support ARM64 (Raspberry Pi, Apple Silicon server) with tested Docker images?
Is there a self-hoster community channel (forum, Discord thread) for sharing configurations?
What is the hardest part of self-hosting, and has it been documented with a working workaround?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full self-hosting domain. A self-host fan needs complete control — every gap that forces cloud dependency is a blocker. Fan perspective: 'Can I run this on my own server with zero Solace cloud dependency?'

floors.score_prior: 6/10 (DECENT — 87+ routes, OpenAPI docs; missing SDK, code samples, Postman collection)
questions: Does the API have a published SDK in at least Python and JavaScript with installation docs?
Is there a Postman or Bruno collection covering the most common API flows?
Do the API docs include runnable code examples (not just parameter descriptions)?
Is the API versioned with a clear semver policy and deprecation notice process?
Does the API have a sandbox mode for testing without affecting production data?
Can a developer obtain an API key from the dashboard without contacting support?
Are rate limits documented with exact headers (X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining)?
Does the API return structured error codes (not just HTTP status) with machine-readable error types?
Is there a changelog listing all API changes per version with migration guides?
Does the API support webhooks for event-driven integration (recipe completed, evidence sealed)?
Is the OpenAPI spec machine-parseable for generating client libraries in any language?
Are all 87+ routes in the OpenAPI spec, or are some undocumented?
Does the API have an uptime page or status page for API fans who build on it?
Is there a developer newsletter or RSS feed for API change notifications?
What is the hardest API integration scenario, and does it work without source code access?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full developer API domain. API fans are prospective platform builders — treat every gap as a potential third-party app that was never built. Fan perspective: 'Can I build a business on top of this API?'

floors.score_prior: 3/10 (POOR — stillwater OSS, solace-browser FSL, solace-cli private; core is not contributable)
questions: Can an OSS fan contribute code to the core product (solace-cli) via pull request?
Is the FSL license for solace-browser clearly explained in plain language on the repository?
Does the CONTRIBUTING.md file explain how to set up a development environment?
Is there a 'good first issue' label on GitHub for new contributors?
Does the stillwater component have enough tests that a new contributor can safely make changes?
Is the OSS fan's contribution acknowledged publicly (in release notes, CONTRIBUTORS.md)?
Can an OSS fan see the roadmap to know where contributions are most needed?
Is there a public GitHub Discussions or community forum where OSS fans can propose features?
Does the project have a code of conduct that makes OSS fans feel safe contributing?
Is the git history clean enough that an OSS fan can understand the evolution of the codebase?
Does the project accept recipe contributions even if the core CLI is private?
Is there a clear boundary documented between what is open and what is private, and why?
Can an OSS fan build a competing product using the stillwater OSS component as a base?
Is there a developer certification or recognition for significant OSS contributors?
What is the single most impactful contribution an OSS fan could make today, and is it documented?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full open source contributor domain. OSS fans are multipliers — every one who contributes brings their network. Fan perspective: 'Is there a meaningful way for me to contribute to this project?'

floors.score_prior: 2.5/10 (POOR — no SOC2, no SSO, no SLA, no customer references; nothing to champion)
questions: Is there a SOC2 Type II audit report available under NDA for enterprise champions to share internally?
Does the product support SSO (SAML 2.0, OIDC) for enterprise identity management?
Is there a published SLA with uptime commitment and response time guarantees?
Does the enterprise plan include a dedicated account manager or support channel?
Is there a security questionnaire (SIG, CAIQ) pre-filled for enterprise vendor assessments?
Does the product have a GDPR Data Processing Agreement (DPA) template for EU enterprises?
Is there at least one enterprise customer reference available for due diligence calls?
Does the product support audit log export in SIEM-compatible format (Splunk, Elastic)?
Is there a penetration test report available for enterprise security reviews?
Does the enterprise plan include on-premise deployment with air-gap support?
Is there a role-based access control (RBAC) system for enterprise team management?
Does the product have ISO 27001 certification or a path to certification documented?
Is there a named security contact for vulnerability disclosure from enterprise customers?
Does the enterprise plan include quarterly business reviews with usage data and roadmap preview?
What is the hardest enterprise compliance question, and does the product answer it with documentation?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full enterprise champion domain. An enterprise fan needs ammunition to champion the product in a procurement review. Fan perspective: 'Can I get this product approved by our security and procurement teams?'

floors.score_prior: 2.5/10 (POOR — no tutorials, no video, no getting-started guide; nothing to remix)
questions: Is there a step-by-step tutorial that a new user can complete in under 30 minutes?
Does the product have video walkthroughs on YouTube or Loom for visual learners?
Is there an interactive tutorial (Jupyter notebook, browser-based REPL) for hands-on learning?
Can a tutorial fan remix an existing tutorial and submit it back to the community?
Are tutorials versioned so fans can find tutorials that match their installed version?
Is there a tutorial for each major use case (Gmail, LinkedIn, scheduling, evidence)?
Does the tutorial progression go from beginner to expert with clear milestones?
Is there a 'Hello World' recipe tutorial that can be completed in under 5 minutes?
Does the tutorial content acknowledge common mistakes and how to recover from them?
Is there a tutorial community where fans share their own learning paths and tips?
Does the documentation have a 'Did this help?' feedback mechanism to improve tutorials?
Is there a cheat sheet or quick reference card for the most common commands and patterns?
Does the tutorial track progress so a fan can resume where they left off?
Is there a tutorial certification or badge for fans who complete the full learning path?
What is the single tutorial that would convert the most new users, and does it exist?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full learning content domain. Tutorial fans are content multipliers — each tutorial they create reduces the support burden. Fan perspective: 'Is there enough content for me to learn this, and can I contribute my own learning?'

floors.score_prior: 2.5/10 (POOR — no beta program, no feedback form, no changelog newsletter; no channel)
questions: Is there a public beta program page where early adopters can sign up for pre-release access?
Does the beta program have a dedicated Discord channel or Slack workspace for beta testers?
Is there a structured beta feedback form (not just GitHub issues) for collecting user experiences?
Does the changelog have a newsletter or RSS feed so beta fans can track every change?
Are beta features clearly flagged in the UI with an opt-in toggle, not silent rollouts?
Do beta testers receive credit or recognition in release notes for specific bug reports?
Is there a beta release cadence (weekly, biweekly) that beta fans can depend on?
Does the beta program have a defined graduation path from beta to stable?
Can beta fans vote on which features should be promoted from beta to stable?
Is there a beta-specific documentation section that acknowledges instability honestly?
Does the beta program offer any incentive (early access, free Pro tier, GLOW points) to attract quality testers?
Is there a public bug tracker where beta fans can see the status of issues they reported?
Does the product have a feature flag system that allows beta fans to test individual features independently?
Is there a beta alumni community that maintains connection after graduation to stable?
What is the highest-value beta test a fan could run today, and is there a structured way to report results?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full early adopter domain. Beta fans are the quality filter before the public release. Fan perspective: 'Is there a meaningful way to be an early adopter and have my feedback actually influence the product?'

floors.score_prior: 1.5/10 (VERY POOR — zero mobile; desktop-only is deliberate architectural decision)
questions: Is the deliberate desktop-only decision documented in the architecture decision record with reasoning?
Is there a mobile-friendly web view for monitoring running automations from a phone?
Can a mobile fan approve a pending automation action via a push notification?
Is there a Progressive Web App (PWA) that provides core monitoring features on mobile?
Does the solaceagi.com dashboard render usably on a 375px mobile viewport?
Is there a companion mobile app for notifications and approvals (even if not for full automation)?
Does the product have a documented plan for mobile support, or is exclusion permanent?
Can a mobile fan start a scheduled automation from their phone and trust it to complete?
Is there a mobile-accessible evidence viewer for reviewing audit trails on the go?
Does the API support mobile OAuth3 flows (PKCE) for mobile app developers to build on?
Is the exclusion of mobile fans acknowledged on the product page so they are not surprised?
Does the roadmap clearly state when (if ever) mobile support will be considered?
Is there an SMS or push notification system for alerting users when an automation needs approval?
Can a mobile fan at minimum view the recipe gallery and documentation on mobile?
What is the minimum mobile experience that would satisfy 80% of mobile fan use cases?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full mobile usage domain. Mobile fans who are excluded should at least understand why and when it might change. Fan perspective: 'Am I explicitly excluded, or just not supported yet?'

floors.score_prior: 1/10 (VERY POOR — no benchmarks, no dashboard, no comparison data; fans cannot advocate without numbers)
questions: Is there a public benchmark comparing recipe replay speed against LLM-based execution?
Does the product publish startup time, memory usage, and CPU usage for standard workloads?
Is there a performance dashboard showing recipe execution time over version history?
Does the CLI have a --benchmark flag for measuring the performance of specific operations?
Are the 81 recipes profiled so fans can see which are fast vs. slow and why?
Is there a comparison table showing Solace vs. Zapier vs. Browser-Use on a standardized benchmark?
Does the product document the performance cost of evidence chain accumulation over time?
Is there a performance regression test suite that runs on every release?
Does the product expose timing headers (X-Duration-Ms) on all API responses?
Is there documentation explaining the performance model (LLM once at preview, CPU replay)?
Does the product have a performance tuning guide for users who need maximum throughput?
Is there a way for performance fans to submit benchmark results and have them aggregated?
Does the recipe engine support parallelism, and is the parallelism overhead documented?
Is there a memory profiling mode for identifying recipe steps that are resource-intensive?
What is the worst-case performance scenario, and is it documented with mitigation strategies?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full speed optimization domain. Performance fans need numbers, not claims. Fan perspective: 'Can I cite specific benchmarks when recommending this product to a performance-conscious colleague?'

floors.score_prior: 0.5/10 (ABSENT — no forum, no Discord, no Slack, no mailing list, no discussion space)
questions: Is there a Discord server, Slack workspace, or forum where community fans can meet each other?
Is there a community code of conduct that makes new members feel safe participating?
Does the community have a welcome ritual for new members (introduction thread, greeter bot)?
Is there a community moderation team or is it solo-founder-only governance?
Does the community have dedicated channels for different topics (recipes, bugs, showcase, off-topic)?
Is there a community showcase where fans can share what they have built?
Does the community feed into the product roadmap (feature requests that get implemented)?
Is there a community call or AMA where fans can ask the founder questions directly?
Does the community have a newsletter summarizing the best discussions and contributions?
Is there a community leaderboard recognizing the most helpful members each month?
Can a community fan search past discussions to find answers without asking duplicate questions?
Does the community have onboarding resources so new members know where to start?
Is the community accessible to non-English speakers with translated resources or dedicated channels?
Does the community generate enough activity that a new member gets a response within 24 hours?
What is the single most important community feature that would convert a lurker into an active member?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full community member domain. Community Fan is the most critical floor in the tower — community infrastructure unlocks 35 other floors. Fan perspective: 'Is there a place for me to belong in this ecosystem?'

floors.score_prior: 7.5/10 (GOOD — 81 recipes cover major platforms; missing marketplace equivalent)
questions: Is there a Zapier or Make integration that allows non-technical fans to connect Solace to their tools?
Does the recipe library cover the top 20 apps in the user's likely stack (Notion, Airtable, HubSpot)?
Is there a recipe request form where fans can request integrations for specific platforms?
Can an integration fan see which integrations are most requested by the community?
Is there an integration marketplace or directory beyond the 81 pre-built recipes?
Does the recipe system support API integrations (not just browser automation) for apps with public APIs?
Is there a template recipe for connecting to any REST API without writing code?
Does the product support native integrations (not browser-automated) for major platforms that have APIs?
Is the recipe contribution process fast enough that a new integration can be submitted and reviewed within a week?
Can an integration fan create a recipe for an app that requires authentication without hardcoding credentials?
Does the recipe system support pagination for fetching large datasets from third-party APIs?
Is there a recipe testing harness so integration fans can verify recipes work before sharing?
Does the product have a 'recipe broken' reporting mechanism for when a site updates its structure?
Is there an integration health dashboard showing which recipes are currently working vs. broken?
What is the most-requested integration that does not yet exist, and what is blocking it?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full third-party connections domain. Integration fans create the ecosystem that retains all other fans. Fan perspective: 'Can I automate the specific tools I already use every day?'

floors.score_prior: 7/10 (GOOD — ALCOA+ evidence, hash-chained, Part 11; missing SOC2, HIPAA, external audit)
questions: Is there an external SOC2 Type II audit report available for compliance fans to review?
Does the evidence chain implementation have a formal write-up mapping to 21 CFR Part 11 requirements?
Is there a HIPAA Business Associate Agreement (BAA) template for healthcare compliance fans?
Does the product have a documented data retention policy with configurable retention periods?
Is there a GDPR compliance documentation package (DPIA, privacy notice, controller/processor boundary)?
Does the evidence chain support tamper-evident chain of custody for regulatory submissions?
Is there a compliance FAQ covering HIPAA, SOC2, ISO 27001, FedRAMP requirements?
Does the product have a named compliance officer or contact for regulatory questions?
Is there a compliance-focused onboarding path that sets up evidence chains and audit trails first?
Does the product support geographic data residency (EU-only, US-only storage options)?
Is there a regulatory submission guide for fans in FDA, EMA, or other regulated environments?
Does the audit trail satisfy the three-part test: contemporaneous, original, attributable?
Is there a compliance dashboard that monitors evidence chain integrity continuously?
Does the product generate compliance-ready exports (PDF reports, structured XML) for regulatory filings?
What is the hardest compliance question from a regulated industry, and does the product answer it with evidence?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full regulatory adherence domain. Compliance fans in regulated industries are the highest-value fan segment — they have budget and mandate. Fan perspective: 'Can I use this in a regulated environment without creating a compliance liability?'

floors.score_prior: 6/10 (DECENT — local-first, git-backed, CLI; missing monitoring, alerting, log rotation, systemd)
questions: Is there a systemd service file or launchd plist for running Solace as a system service?
Does the product expose a /healthz or /readyz endpoint for load balancer and monitoring use?
Is there a log rotation configuration (logrotate compatible) to prevent log files from filling disk?
Does the CLI have a --log-level flag (debug, info, warning, error) for verbose diagnostics?
Is there a metrics endpoint (Prometheus-compatible) for scraping system health data?
Does the product have an alerting mechanism (email, webhook) when a recipe fails or evidence chain is corrupted?
Is there a backup script or procedure for preserving the vault and evidence chain?
Does the product support centralized configuration management (Ansible, Chef, Puppet) for fleet deployment?
Is there an upgrade path that does not require manual intervention (auto-update or update-in-place)?
Does the product have an audit event that fires when configuration changes are made?
Is there a resource usage dashboard showing CPU, memory, and disk consumption per recipe?
Does the CLI have a diagnostic command (solace doctor) that checks the system health and reports issues?
Is the git-backed storage compatible with automated backup tools (restic, borgbackup, rsync)?
Does the product support running multiple isolated instances on the same server (multi-tenant sysadmin)?
What is the hardest sysadmin operational scenario, and does the product have a documented runbook?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full system management domain. SysAdmin fans run the infrastructure that enables all other fans. Fan perspective: 'Can I deploy, monitor, maintain, and recover this product in a production environment?'

floors.score_prior: 6/10 (DECENT — local-first, BYOK, evidence chains; missing third-party safety audit)
questions: Is the source code available for independent security review (at minimum for the solace-browser FSL component)?
Is there a third-party safety audit or red team report verifying the AI safety claims?
Does the product have a documented model for what the AI can and cannot do without human approval?
Is the YinYang approval dialog designed to prevent approval fatigue (not just displaying 'Are you sure?')?
Does the evidence chain capture what the AI WOULD HAVE done as well as what it DID do?
Is there a kill switch that halts all AI activity immediately without requiring network access?
Does the product quantify how often users override AI suggestions (as an honesty signal)?
Is there a documented boundary between AI-assisted and fully-automated operations?

Is there a partial restore option (restore only evidence chains, not vault credentials)?
Does the product have a backup retention policy suggestion (how many backups to keep, when to prune)?
Is the backup format documented as an open format that Backup Fans can independently verify?
Does the product have a 'backup before upgrade' prompt that prevents data loss during version upgrades?
Is there a Backup Fan community guide with tested recipes for integration with common backup tools (restic, Backblaze)?
What is the worst-case data loss scenario, and is there a tested recovery procedure for it?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full data backup and restore domain. Backup fans catch critical gaps — a product that claims data sovereignty but has no backup command has not finished the promise. Fan perspective: 'Can I recover everything if my machine dies today?'

questions: Write the viral tweet about Solace backed by code, not claims — and verify every word against the codebase.

floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full evangelism domain.
